concerns.text: FR-006 mandates a sensitivity analysis sweeping thresholds over {0.01, 0.05, 0.1} standard deviations. The idea's methodology sketch (step 9) only mentions 'exclusion of extreme temperature outliers' without specifying these exact numeric thresholds. While robustness is required, hard-coding specific sweep values in the spec without an explicit request in the idea constitutes over-reach on implementation details. The spec should state 'sweep over a range of standard deviations' and defer specific values to the implementation phase.

User Story 1 Acceptance Scenario 1 requires 'at least 95% of the original records' to be matched. The idea's assumptions section states 'at least 90% of the records' will have sufficient granularity. The spec's 95% target is a stricter empirical claim not present in the idea. Per the 'Measurable Outcomes' guidance in the spec template, specific percentages should be deferred to the research phase. This is an over-reach on a success metric.
